title: Kanmaniye Pesu Lyrics Kaakki Sattai (1985)
stanzas:
kannangal pudhu roja poo
un kangal iru oodhaa poo
idhu poovil pooththa
poovaiyo

andhapuram indhapuramo
vizhi maiyittu andhikalai
sollitharumo iru kai thottu.(2)

aayiram pon pokkum
en dhegam engumey
angulangidaamal
inba gangai pongumey.

tholilil en maarbilum
konjidum en anjugam
naan nee yedhu hoi hoi

kannangal pudhu roja poo
en kangal iru oodhaa poo
idhu poovil pooththa
poovaiye

unnai kodu ennai tharuven
oru thaalattil pilli Tamizh
sollitharuven vizhi moodamal.(2)

kangalaal en dhegam
engum kaayam seigiraai.
kaigalaal en paadham
neevi aara seigiraai.

vaanagam ivvaiyagam
yaavume en ...
nee thaan thanthaai ho ho

kannangal pudhu roja poo
un kangal iru oodhaa poo
idhu poovil pooththa
poovaiyo

enrichedMetadata.faq:
Frequently Asked Questions

What is the theme of Kanmaniye Pesu?

The song is a romantic melody that explores themes of love and unspoken emotions.

Who are the singers of Kanmaniye Pesu?

The song is sung by S. P. Balasubrahmanyam and S. Janaki.

What is the context of the song in the movie Kaaki Sattai?

The song is set in a romantic context, highlighting the tender moments between the lead characters.

Who composed the music for Kaaki Sattai?

The music for the movie was composed by Ilaiyaraaja.

When was Kaaki Sattai released?

The movie was released in 1985.
enrichedMetadata.summary: Kanmaniye Pesu is a romantic duet from the 1985 film Kaaki Sattai, capturing the essence of love and unspoken emotions. Sung by the legendary duo S. P. Balasubrahmanyam and S. Janaki, the song is a soothing melody that evokes nostalgia and warmth. The lyrics beautifully convey the tender moments shared between the lead characters, making it a perfect choice for romantic occasions. Ilaiyaraaja's music adds a timeless quality to this classic, ensuring its place in the hearts of listeners.
enrichedMetadata.high_ctr_intro: Kanmaniye Pesu from the movie Kaaki Sattai is a timeless romantic melody that captures the essence of love with its soothing tunes. The magical voices of S. P. Balasubrahmanyam and S. Janaki, combined with Ilaiyaraaja's enchanting music, create an unforgettable experience. Dive into this classic duet that beautifully expresses unspoken emotions and tender moments.
stanzaMeanings: Speak, my dear, what does your silence say?
Your eyes are like fresh roses, your eyes are like two purple flowers, is this the flower that bloomed in this garden?
Speak, my dear, what does your silence say?
Is that place or this place, with eyes closed, will the evening tell us, touching with both hands?
A thousand golds will flow, my body everywhere, without stopping, the river of joy flows.
On my shoulder and chest, you cuddle, my dear, what are you and I, hoi hoi
Speak, my dear, what does your silence say?
Your eyes are like fresh roses, my eyes are like two purple flowers, is this the flower that bloomed in this garden?
Speak, my dear, what does your silence say?
Give yourself, I will give myself, in a lullaby, I will tell you sweet Tamil words without closing my eyes.
With your eyes, you hurt my body everywhere. With your hands, you soothe my feet.
The sky, this world, everything is mine... you are the one who gave it, ho ho
Speak, my dear, what does your silence say?
Your eyes are like fresh roses, your eyes are like two purple flowers, is this the flower that bloomed in this garden?
Speak, my dear, what does your silence say?
